rationale_for_baseline_tier: A systemically well, afebrile aged-care resident with localised tense bullae and no infection signs needs prompt (24-48 h) in-person skin review and topical therapy with nurse monitoring — a T2 baseline. Frailty and secondary-infection risk justify prompt (not routine-week) review, and a clear cellulitis safety-net escalating to urgent assessment/transfer.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, spreading hot painful erythema, purulent/cloudy blister fluid, or systemic decline
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange urgent assessment/ED transfer same day
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates secondary cellulitis/sepsis in a frail host needing antibiotics and possible admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she develops a fever, the redness spreads and becomes hot and painful, or the blister fluid turns cloudy, arrange urgent medical assessment or hospital transfer.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail older adult in residential care
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for in-person review and infection escalation; leverage on-site nursing for monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like an autoimmune blistering condition, not an infection. Start the steroid ointment, keep the blisters intact and legs elevated, and have the GP review within a day or two. Nurses should watch for fever, spreading hot redness, or cloudy blister fluid and escalate if any appear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: In-person GP/skin review within 2-3 days; urgent assessment if infection signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Facility nursing staff to escalate to on-call/ED if infection or systemic decline overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of localised bullous pemphigoid in an aged-care resident; cellulitis screened negative; topical steroid started; nurse monitoring and biopsy/dermatology referral advised.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating as trivial with no infection safety-net or timely review in a frail patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A secondary cellulitis is missed and progresses to sepsis in a frail resident.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T2 with monitoring is correct; risk is failing to escalate on infection signs.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Antibiotics/ED transfer without infection signs is over-triage.
